summary: Machine Learning Engineer with hands-on experience designing, training, and deploying production ML systems across computer vision, NLP, and retrieval-augmented generation. Practiced in interpretability techniques including SHAP and Captum attribution, evaluation metric design, and MLOps tooling including Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines. Collaborative by nature, with experience translating real-world workflows into scalable ML-driven solutions.

skills.ml: XGBoost, DistilBERT, YOLO, LangChain, RAG, SHAP, Captum (XAI), HuggingFace, OpenAI API, Claude (Sonnet/Haiku), Deepgram, Scikit-learn, PyTorch
skills.infra: Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, CI/CD, Railway, Supabase (PostgreSQL), GCP, Vercel, MLOps

experience.title: Deep Learning Intern
bullets: Designed and implemented an end-to-end ML pipeline covering training, evaluation, and deployment of a YOLO-based object detection model in Python, improving classification accuracy by 30% and eliminating a fully manual counting process for industrial pipe inventory.

Built a data augmentation pipeline using NumPy and Pandas, scaling the training dataset from a few hundred to 10,000+ images and manually re-annotating 500+ edge cases; accuracy improved from 65% to 85.4% on held-out evaluation data.
Containerized the model with Docker and deployed on Kubernetes for horizontal scaling of model inference, enabling the team to handle variable load without code changes.
Designed normalized relational database schemas in SQL to store image metadata and inference results, enabling cross-team queries on model performance and inventory trends.

projects.name: AI Interview Engine
projects.subtitle: Voice-First Candidate Screening Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Express, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, Deepgram, Supabase, Railway, Vercel
bullets: Designed a multi-turn conversational AI engine with dynamic context injection: the LLM receives each candidate's parsed resume before the session and generates targeted, role-specific follow-up questions in real time, demonstrating applied experience translating domain workflows into ML-driven solutions.

Built an automated document ingestion pipeline (PDF parsing, Claude Haiku, MD5 deduplication caching) and a post-interview evaluation system scoring candidates across 4 dimensions with advance/hold/reject output, including defined evaluation metrics and validation criteria.

Engineered a real-time voice pipeline integrating Deepgram Nova-2 (speech-to-text) and Aura (text-to-speech) with Claude Sonnet 4.6 (large language model), achieving 4-5s end-to-end latency per conversational turn and replacing human interviewers for initial candidate screening.
Deployed full-stack on Railway, Vercel, and Supabase (PostgreSQL with Row-Level Security and Magic Link authentication) with Brevo SMTP for transactional email automation.

projects.name: CodeSyntax AI
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack RAG Developer Assistant
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, GitHub Actions
bullets: Built a production retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system using GPT-4o-mini with semantic search over Python Docs, RealPython, and StackOverflow, indexed in Pinecone via OpenAI vector embeddings; leveraged LangChain as the AI orchestration framework throughout.

Implemented an LLM-based query intent classifier and result re-ranker by source authority, plus multi-turn conversation support via LangChain chat-history condensing, both measurably reducing off-topic responses.

Maintained 35+ pytest unit tests and a 55-question domain evaluation set; GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline builds the Docker image and deploys to Railway (API) and Vercel (frontend) on every merge to main.

projects.name: AuthentiText
projects.subtitle: AI-Generated Text Detection Engine
projects.stack: Python, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, GCP, Docker
bullets: Incorporated interpretability and explainability techniques including SHAP feature importance and Captum token-level attribution heatmaps into the final model, providing transparent, explainable predictions in line with XAI best practices.

Trained a weighted ensemble model combining XGBoost (21 hand-crafted linguistic features: perplexity, burstiness, lexical density) with a fine-tuned DistilBERT transformer, achieving 99.1% F1 and 99.96% AUROC on held-out test data.

Conducted systematic model comparison across four architectures (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, DistilBERT) using ROC curves and confusion matrices to define evaluation metrics and validate model selection.
Trained on the merged HC3 corpus spanning Wikipedia, Reddit, medicine, and finance domains; deployed as a publicly accessible machine learning inference app on HuggingFace Spaces.
